title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 8)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: In Arlington, a one-family ranch on Wellesley Road sold for $880,000; it has 3 bedrooms and 1 bath.
Boston's luxury condo at 1 Dalton St. was sold for $14 million, the highest sale in this report.
In Belmont, a two-family old-style home on Chester Road sold for $1,210,000, featuring 4 bedrooms and 2 baths.

title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville on Newbury Street often has lines due to its unique 'one size fits all' clothing policy.
Blank Street coffee on Boylston Street regularly needs line security due to its popularity.
Brick Street Bagels in South Boston features long lines, driven by strong social media praise.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli, 40, of Stoughton, pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for spa injections without a license.
Fadanelli imported counterfeit Botox and fillers from China and Brazil, earning more than $1 million from clients.
Fadanelli faces up to 20 years in prison and fines for importing illegal merchandise and using counterfeit products.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jacob Graves, 29, was fatally shot by Boston police outside his Hemenway Street apartment during a mental health crisis.
Graves attacked officers and responders with a 'sword-like weapon' after a distress call, injuring several, officials reported.
The shooting happened near Northeastern University amidst recent local violence, prompting protests over communication from the administration.

title: Celtics’ Brad Stevens addresses ‘tiresome’ college basketball rumors
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Brad Stevens dismissed rumors of leaving the Celtics for college basketball roles, including a recent link to UNC.
Stevens confirmed his commitment to the Celtics, emphasizing satisfaction with his role and team's support.
Celtics, under Stevens' management, are currently positioned second in the Eastern Conference preparing for playoffs.

title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix, 29, sentenced to life with parole possibility after 15 years for 2024 murder of Maceo Martin in Boston.
Lapaix was convicted of second-degree murder for fatally shooting Martin during a dispute outside a Cambridge apartment building.
Victim Maceo Martin, 41, died at Boston Medical Center after being shot in the chest with a rifle.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if her trial is split into two phases, her defense states.
Clancy's defense requested a trial split to separate proof of the killings from her mental state; the judge denied this.
Prosecutors allege Clancy strangled her children in 2023; her trial is set to begin on July 20 in Plymouth Superior Court.

title: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots traded linebacker Marte Mapu to Houston Texans for a 2027 sixth-round pick and a 2027 seventh-round pick.
Mapu, drafted in the third round of the 2023 NFL Draft, played mostly on special teams for New England.
The trade frees up $1.5 million in cap space and a roster spot for the Patriots.

title: Suspicious person prompts lockdown, canceled classes at R.I. college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Community College of Rhode Island's Warwick campus was locked down due to a suspicious man reportedly with a weapon.
Warwick police used K-9 units and drones to search for the suspect, finding a backpack with airsoft and pellet guns.
Classes were canceled for the day; police have identified the suspect but not yet located him, deeming no threat to locals.

title: Red Sox get more bad news after Triston Casas suffers setback in his recovery
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Triston Casas' recovery from a torn left patellar tendon is stalled due to a rib muscle strain.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora confirmed Casas' attempted return to hitting was unsuccessful, prompting further imaging.
Casas, hindered by injuries since 2024, has played only 92 games since his breakout 2023 season.
